title: Rewrite an Oban drain-queue test to use perform_job + Oban.Testing
prompt: Rewrite the Oban test at `test_fixtures/oban_real_queue_test.exs` to `test/my_app/mailer/digest_worker_test.exs`. Requirements: (1) `use MyApp.DataCase, async: true`, (2) `use Oban.Testing, repo: MyApp.Repo`, (3) create two users — one opted-in, one opted-out, (4) use `perform_job(DigestWorker, %{"user_id" => alice.id})` to execute the worker synchronously in-process (which CAN see sandbox data), (5) assert the email was delivered. Must NOT use `Oban.drain_queue/1` or `Oban.insert()`. Explain in a comment why drain_queue would not work with sandbox.
tier_rationale: Requires understanding WHY drain_queue fails (sandbox commit-visibility), not just how to use perform_job. Sonnet often fixes the symptom without explaining the cause.
research_citation: "Sandbox transactions never commit. If they never commit, then the rows are never visible to other processes, and that means they are never visible to Oban." — Elixir Forum, benwilson512
